title: DIY Modern Farmhouse Nightstand
summary: A nightstand with visible 2x2 corner posts framing plywood side and back panels, a plywood top and shelf, and a single drawer on a euro slide.
description:
Unlike this catalog's enclosed-box or angled-finger-pull nightstands, this one is built as an open 2x2 stick frame with plywood panels applied to the sides and back — the legs stay genuinely visible at every corner rather than being hidden inside a closed carcass. Build the front frame and the remaining frame pieces first, then join them into the full leg-and-rail skeleton before adding any panels.

The plywood side and back panels, plus the top and shelf, all attach to this frame rather than forming the structure themselves — that's the key difference from a plywood-box nightstand, where the panels ARE the structure. A single drawer rides on an 18" euro-style slide with 1/2" clearance, sized to fit the front opening.

The drawer face is a plain 1x6 or plywood panel, attached last once the drawer box is confirmed to run smoothly.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, at least 18 in x 15-1/4 in
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood, at least 18 in x 15-1/4 in

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Assemble the front frame, legs, and remaining frame pieces into the full leg-and-rail skeleton.

steps.title: Add the panels
steps.body: Attach the plywood side, back, top, and shelf panels to the frame.

steps.title: Install the drawer slide
steps.body: Mount the euro-style drawer slide inside the front opening.

steps.title: Build the drawer
steps.body: Assemble the drawer box from its sides, front/back, and plywood bottom, fit it to the slide, and attach the drawer face.
materials: 1/4" plywood, at least 18 in x 15-1/4 in

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Modern farmhouse nightstand with visible corner posts
